title: 10 best herbal creams for varicose veins

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with varicose veins because they contain natural compounds that promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in grapes, such as resveratrol, help to strengthen blood vessels and improve their flexibility, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins like pain, swelling, and discoloration. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of grape creams may also help to reduce bruising and discomfort associated with varicose veins, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Grape creams contain high levels of vitamin C, which helps to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the veins, alleviating symptoms of varicose veins.

Resveratrol: Resveratrol, a polyphenol found in grape skins, has potent antioxidant properties that help to strengthen vein walls, improve elasticity, and reduce oxidative stress, all of which can contribute to the development of varicose veins.

Potassium: Grape creams are rich in potassium, an essential mineral that helps to relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and promote healthy circulation, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins.
Barks: The barks of the grape vine contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine the dried botanicals with 100ml of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard at room temperature for 14 days to infuse.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard solids after 2 hours.
Mix 50ml of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil then heat at low temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with varicose veins because of their unique blend of natural ingredients. The cream's active compounds, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected areas. This helps to alleviate symptoms of varicose veins, including swelling, pain, and discoloration. Additionally, witch hazel's astringent properties help to constrict and strengthen blood vessels, promoting healthier and more elastic veins overall.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in witch hazel cream help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and relieving pressure on varicose veins, which can alleviate discomfort and pain.
Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of witch hazel cream help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to vein damage and varicose veins.

Astringent Properties: Witch hazel's astringent properties help reduce inflammation and shrink the appearance of damaged or weakened blood vessels, promoting healthier-looking skin and reducing the visibility of varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are harvested for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with varicose veins.
Roots: The roots are used to create a soothing cream that calms inflammation and reduces pain in the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hamamelis virginiana bark, drying it thoroughly for at least two weeks before use.
Combine 10 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana bark with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-proof container.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a tincture.
Strain and discard the solids, then mix the tincture with 200 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with varicose veins because of its impressive ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and strengthen blood vessels. The cream's potent flavonoids and antioxidants work together to alleviate discomfort, swelling, and discoloration associated with varicose veins. As the skin absorbs these beneficial compounds, it becomes more resilient and supple, allowing for improved elasticity and reduced appearance of spider veins.
remedy_properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula helps to reduce swelling and inflammation associated with varicose veins, promoting healing and relieving discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold cream's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessel walls, contributing to the formation of varicose veins, and help to protect against further damage.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula's natural wound-healing properties accelerate tissue repair and promote healthy skin regeneration, which is particularly beneficial for fragile skin around varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and soothe discomfort associated with varicose veins.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, which improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them using a low-temperature oven or air drying method for 24 hours.
Mix 20% dried calendula flowers with 80% sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c to 70°c for 6 hours allowing the calendula extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Whip the infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler at 60°c to 70°c for 10 minutes until a smooth cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant creams helps with varicose veins because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as turmeric and ginger, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease discomfort and swelling associated with varicose veins. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of unsightly bulges and discoloration. Regular use can leave legs feeling smoother, softer, and more confident.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Curcuminoids in the curry plant have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with varicose veins.

Antioxidant activity: The essential oils of the curry plant contain antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to vein health issues like varicose veins.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Some compounds in the curry plant have been shown to constrict blood vessels, which could potentially help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as swelling and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective in soothing varicose veins.
Rhyzomes: Having a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discoloration associated with varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried helichrysum italicum flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 20g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for 4 hours stirring occasionally to infuse.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax pellets and 2ml of vitamin e oil heating gently over low heat until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with varicose veins because of its unique ability to promote blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen vessel walls. The cream's active ingredients, such as echinacea and calendula, work together to improve overall vascular health, allowing for smoother blood flow and reducing the appearance of unsightly varicosities. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to varicose vein formation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Relieves swelling, pain, and discoloration associated with varicose veins by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation.

Vasoactive compounds: Stimulates the dilation of blood vessels, improves microcirculation, and enhances the removal of waste products from affected areas, which can help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin around varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia roots in sufficient quantities for the desired batch size.
Grind 20g of dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered echinacea with 100ml of sweet almond oil and heat gently over low temperature.
Steep the mixture for 6 hours before filtering through cheesecloth to remove solids and reserve the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil, heating until fully incorporated and cooled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with varicose veins because it contains the bioactive compound absinthin, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the affected area, absinthin reduces inflammation and improves blood circulation, alleviating the symptoms of varicose veins such as swelling, pain, and discomfort. Additionally, wormwood's vasoconstrictive properties help to reduce the appearance of bulging veins by constricting blood vessels, promoting a smoother skin texture and reducing the visible signs of varicose veins.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with varicose veins, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall vascular health, which can help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as discoloration and swelling.

Vasodilatory effects: The bioactive compounds in wormwood cream have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, meaning they help dilate blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which can help reduce the appearance of varicose veins by reducing pressure on affected veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Barks: Contain tannins that aid in strengthening blood vessels and improving overall vein health.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to decarboxylate and infuse the oil.
Strain the hot oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of coconut oil until smooth, then add the infused oil and mix well until a creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with varicose veins because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties that help to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with varicose veins. The creams also improve circulation by strengthening blood vessel walls and enhancing lymphatic drainage, which helps to alleviate the visible appearance of twisted and enlarged veins. Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation around affected areas, reducing the appearance of varicose veins.

Vasodilatory property: Dandelion's vasodilatory effects can help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can reduce pressure on varicose veins and alleviate symptoms such as pain and heaviness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it effective in treating varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from organic sources in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Dry the harvested plants using a warm air circulation method for at least 24 hours.
Combine one part dried taraxacum officinale flowers with two parts sweet almond oil and beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and heating it to 160°f over medium heat for 15 minutes.
Strain the melted mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with varicose veins because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms associated with varicose veins such as pain, swelling, and discoloration. Barks: Contain triterpenoid saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl and heat it at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Combine the cooled extract with 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 75°c and holds its shape when dropped, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with varicose veins because they contain salicin, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and pain. Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salix alba bark extract from reputable sources ensuring potency and purity.
Combine 20 grams of salix alba bark powder with 100 milliliters of organic jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes under gentle stirring to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and sealing tightly.
Allow the cream base to mature at room temperature for 48 hours before whipping with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with varicose veins because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and strengthen blood vessel walls. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves from a trusted source following proper guidelines for collection and handling.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for 6-8 hours or until crumbly texture is achieved.
Combine 50g of dried urtica dioica leaves with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and steep for 30 minutes to infuse herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding solids after 1 hour of steeping time.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2-3 hours before whipping with 10g of beeswax to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with varicose veins because they contain aescin, a powerful bioflavonoid that strengthens and stabilizes blood vessels. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, horse chestnut creams help to alleviate symptoms such as swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with varicose veins. remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried horse chestnut bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered bark with 50 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar and infuse at room temperature for 48 hours.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of carnauba wax in a double boiler and heat until melted in 15 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and stir until smooth and cooled to room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet rush creams helps with varicose veins because they promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The herbal ingredients, such as witch hazel and horse chestnut, work together to strengthen vein walls and improve elasticity, allowing for more efficient blood flow. As a result, the appearance of varicose veins is reduced, and discomfort caused by poor circulation is alleviated. Butcher's Broom (Ruscus aculeatus): Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with varicose veins, while also improving blood circulation.
Hawthorn (Crataegus monogyna): May help improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure, which can contribute to the development of varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Collect 50g of dried petiveria alliacea root, 20g of beeswax, and 100ml of sweet almond oil for the cream base.
Grind the dried petiveria alliacea root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure even extraction.
Combine the ground petiveria alliacea root with 50ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant bowl, allowing it to steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid, then mix with the cream base ingredients.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture achieves a smooth, consistent texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with varicose veins because of its natural ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves at dawn on a sunny day and dry them in a warm oven for 30 minutes.
Combine one part dried crataegus monogyna leaves with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 45 minutes to extract the active compounds from the herbal material, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressing time.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken before whipping it with an electric mixer for five minutes and adding three teaspoons of beeswax for preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with varicose veins because they contain flavonoids, which have a natural ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids in fennel work by constricting blood vessels and strengthening vein walls, reducing the appearance of unsightly bulges and discomfort associated with varicose veins. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds, measuring 100 grams for optimal potency and efficacy.
Dry the foeniculum vulgare seeds in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to enhance bioavailability.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, taking approximately 15 minutes.
Mix the ground foeniculum vulgare with 250 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax, heating the mixture gently for 5-7 minutes until fully incorporated.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over a period of 30-45 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper creams helps with varicose veins because they contain natural ingredients that improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen vein walls. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried juniperus communis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground juniperus communis powder with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then remove from heat for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them at room temperature for at least 30 days to allow the ingredients to mature.
Once mature, gently knead the mixture with a spatula for 1 minute, then use as desired to alleviate varicose vein discomfort and promote circulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with varicose veins because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation. The cream's active ingredient, hypericum perforatum, works by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation in the affected area. Hypotensive action: The active compounds in St. John's Wort cream can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing pressure on the veins and alleviating symptoms of varicose veins.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality hypericum perforatum dried flowers and leaves, measuring out 2 tablespoons per batch.
Combine the gathered herbs with 1 cup of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a gentle boil for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the herb-infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids.
Add 1/2 cup of unrefined coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the strained liquid, stirring until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars, sealing tightly and allowing to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and dry them thoroughly in shade for 2 weeks.
Combine dried flowers with 20% beeswax and 10% coconut oil by weight in double boiler.
Heat mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until beeswax melts completely.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature then strain through cheesecloth into glass jar.
Store cream in airtight container in refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with varicose veins because they contain a combination of soothing herbs that reduce inflammation, calm swelling, and improve circulation. The natural astringent properties of marshmallow root and slippery elm help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of bulging veins. Additionally, the emollient properties of mallow cream moisturize and nourish the skin around varicose veins, improving its tone and texture, while also providing gentle relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilages, which help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with varicose veins.

Vasodilatory properties: Herbal mallow creams contain compounds that can dilate blood vessels, improving blood circulation and reducing the risk of further inflammation and damage to the affected veins.

Emollient and soothing properties: Mallow creams have a moisturizing effect on the skin, providing a protective barrier that helps to reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort caused by varicose veins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a natural adhesive substance that helps soothe and calm inflammation, making it an effective ingredient in creams for reducing vein discomfort.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and saponins, the barks help to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and strengthen blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried malva sylvestris flowers, 100ml of sweet almond oil, and 50g of beeswax.
Combine the dried malva sylvestris flowers with 500ml of boiling water in a large saucepan and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix the cooled liquid with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and blended.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars and let cool before storing at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato creams helps with varicose veins because of their unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The creams contain natural compounds that dilate blood vessels, allowing for a smoother flow of blood back to the heart. This reduced pressure on the veins helps to alleviate discomfort and swelling associated with varicose veins. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of wild potato creams can help to reduce redness and pain in affected areas, promoting overall venous health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Butcher's Broom (Ruscus aculeatus): Helps improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of varicose veins.
Ginkgo biloba: May help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of developing varicose veins due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Horse Chestnut (Aesculus hippocastanum): Has been shown to have a vasoconstrictive effect, which can help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins such as swelling, pain, and discoloration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild potato plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them effective in alleviating symptoms associated with varicose veins.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the wild potato plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by varicose veins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum kok saghyz roots in the early morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and steep them in 500ml of boiling water for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to obtain a decoction, then add 100g of beeswax and heat until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool, whipping occasionally, until it reaches a consistency similar to thick butter.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for varicose veins are natural topical treatments that utilize plant-based ingredients to alleviate symptoms associated with these unsightly and often painful blood vessels. These creams work by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and strengthening vein walls, which helps to reduce the appearance of spider veins and bulging varicose veins. Examples include creams containing witch hazel, horse chestnut extract, and lavender oil, which have been shown to improve skin tone and reduce discomfort associated with varicose veins. For those who suffer from this condition, these herbal creams can be a game-changer, allowing them to feel more confident in their own skin and enjoy improved overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat varicose veins with herbal creams is by applying a combination of potent herbs like Horse Chestnut, Butcher's Broom, and Witch Hazel topically. These herbs help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and strengthen vein walls. Look for creams containing 1-2% concentration of these herbs and apply them twice daily to affected areas. This natural approach can provide relief from discomfort, pain, and unsightly appearance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to varicose veins that are treated with herbal creams are spider veins, telangiectasia, and rosacea. These conditions involve dilation of blood vessels near the surface of the skin, leading to unsightly appearance and discomfort. Herbal creams containing ingredients like horse chestnut, butcher's broom, and chamomile have been traditionally used to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote collagen production, helping to alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and discoloration.
